fullText:
Hypesters sometimes pretend that AGI has already been achieved, but that’s not certainly true by traditional understandings of what that term means.

A new paper from Google DeepMind, Waterloo, ANU and UCL makes clear that what the new paper calls “competent AGI” has NOT yet been achieved, let alone what they are calling “expert” or “superhuman AGI”.

I fully concur; all claims to the contrary are just marketing.

user.name: DAIR.AI
user.description: Democratizing AI research, education, and technologies. Learn about AI Agents for FREE at https://t.co/HHXg8rryu4

fullText:
Outstanding paper on computer-using agents.

(bookmark it)

Computer-using agents drive real software through the screen, but they solve every task from scratch. Ask one to repeat a task, and it re-reads the screen and re-reasons every tap, paying the full cost again.

PreAct compiles the first successful run into a small state-machine program, states that check the screen and transitions that act, then replays it directly on later runs. That runs 8.5 to 13x faster with no per-step language-model calls.

Replay stays guarded. At each step, PreAct checks that the screen matches what the program expects before acting, and hands control back to the agent when reality diverges.

Why does it matter?

Most computer-use costs are repeated reasoning on tasks the agent has already solved. Amortizing that into a replayable program is a clean way to make agents faster the second time.

Paper: https://t.co/kMloX0qC5M

Learn to build effective AI agents in our academy: https://t.co/LRnpZN7L4c

user.name: Garry Tan
user.description: President & CEO @ycombinator —Founder @garryslist—Creator of GStack & GBrain—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop

fullText:
Rough estimate on $ productivity lost by Fable 5 ban: $12M per hour

Frontier AI-coding daily actives, mid-2026: 5M devs 
Fully-loaded cost: $90/hr 

Work routed to Fable in 48 hours: 17.8%
Fable is on average ~15% more productive 

Effective throughput loss per dev = 17.8% × 15% ≈ 2.7% of output

2.7% × $90/hr = ~$2.40/dev/hr × 5M devs = $12M per working hour

fullText:
For data engineers, data scientists, ML engineers, and analysts, context matters. 

A useful AI agent needs to understand not just the code, but the data it depends on, the assets it may affect, and the environment where it will run.

That’s why we built Genie Code. With Genie Code, teams can bring AI-native development into the same platform they use for data engineering, analytics, machine learning, and production AI.

Genie Code uses relevant notebooks, SQL queries, Lakeflow pipelines, Unity Catalog metadata, permissions, lineage, and compute as it works. 

And now it’s more powerful, with new features for complex, multi-threaded work. 

See what's new in Genie Code: https://t.co/lvXKbbvfBG

user.name: Vincent Boucher · AGI.eth → ASI.eth
user.description: President, @Quebec_AI & @Montreal_AI. AGI.eth → ASI.eth. Sovereign Intelligence. Institutional Command. Govern capability before capability governs.

fullText:
AI does not need to produce more output.

It needs to produce proof.

I’m sharing my paper:

GoalOS Mission OS: The Proof OS for Autonomous AI Work

The thesis is simple:

AI creates output.
GoalOS creates proof.

Today’s models can answer.
Agents can act.
But institutions need something harder:

evidence
verification
risk boundaries
source provenance
decision state
rollback conditions
reusable capability

That is the gap GoalOS Mission OS is designed to close.

A normal AI workflow starts with a prompt and ends with a response.

GoalOS starts with an objective and runs until proof is done.

The output is not merely a report.

It is a Governed Decision State:

mission contract
claims matrix
source provenance
contradiction register
Evidence Docket
verifier report
risk ledger
executive brief
decision deck
action graph
Chronicle entry
reusable capability package

The core distinction:

Research assistant = topic → report

Mission OS = objective → proof → decision state → action → capability

That is the shift.

From content generation to proof-to-action work.

From impressive output to institutional trust.

From “the AI said so” to “the evidence package supports this decision.”

The rule is strict:

No proof, no settlement.
No eval, no propagation.
No rollback, no release.

GoalOS also introduces an autonomous publication law:

the website is generated from proof-aligned source,
checked by automation,
opened as a human-review pull request,
and published only after review.

This matters because AI-generated communication is becoming part of institutional reality.

If the public artifact is not tied to evidence, it becomes theater.

If it is proof-aligned, review-gated, and replayable, it becomes infrastructure.

The immediate commercial wedge is a 48-hour proof mission:

give GoalOS one high-stakes objective and receive a proof-backed decision package with evidence, risk, verification, action, and reuse built in.

This is not a claim of achieved AGI, ASI, guaranteed returns, legal advice, financial advice, production certification, or civilization-scale capability.

It is a claim-bounded architecture for turning AI output into governed, auditable, reusable institutional capability.

The next frontier of autonomous AI work is not longer answers.

It is proof-bearing action.

Credit:
Vincent Boucher
President, https://t.co/dCKMr5Lici and https://t.co/9VRSyr4dv4

Paper:
The Proof OS for Autonomous AI Work
https://t.co/ywGw4wqDs9

I’m attaching the first page because the thesis is worth reading directly.

Set the objective.
GoalOS runs until proof is done.

#GoalOS #AGIAlpha #QuebecAI #SovereignAI #ArtificialIntelligence #Agents

fullText:
We are excited to announce the next evolution of Agent Bricks as our agent platform.

Agent Bricks has expanded into a comprehensive platform for building agents with any model and any harness, accessing data anywhere, and confidently deploying and controlling agents in production.

Agent Bricks provides the building blocks developers need to build impactful agents, including secure sandboxes, agent memory, token capacity, and managed infrastructure. 

The platform is open by design, supporting any model, any harness, and any data source. We also offer a managed version of Omnigent, our open source meta-harness, to orchestrate different harnesses.

We believe the future of agents requires data and AI on a single platform, making it easier to build and operate agents in production.

We can't wait to see what you build! https://t.co/EsKDdsqncW

fullText:
Schnarch’s Four Pillars of Balance in relationships

Solid flexible self — knowing who you are without needing agreement.

Quiet mind, calm heart — self-soothing (vs. needing the other to soothe you).

Grounded responding — staying regulated under their reactivity instead of fusing or fleeing. This is literally "say the true small thing and don't move."

Meaningful endurance — tolerating discomfort for growth. The friction tolerance.

fullText:
牛津和斯坦福大学搞的一个让AI当端到端的数据记者，从原始数据一路干到可交互成品报道的工具：Data2Story

找数据、跑统计、选角度、做图表、拟稿排版这些脏活儿累活儿给AI，人工负责定选题、把关创意

把一篇高质量数据新闻从新闻编辑部几周时间压缩到小时级，搞新闻的可以看看

目的不是让AI代替写稿，而是给一个自带审计功能的协作系统，选题你定，它只负责把数据挖透、把证据链焊死、把交互图表做好

它用多智能体系统模拟完整的新闻编辑部workflow

首先它的证据可追溯性，系统会把每一个数字、角度和素材追溯到原始数据、代码或者外部参考文献，确保所有论断都有据可查

最后生成多模态新闻成品，不是有图就行，而是根据内容自动选择最佳载体

比如说讲世界杯各城市气温差异时，生成交互式热力地图；分析音乐音高变化时嵌入音频对比等等

对需要处理大量数据、跑统计、做可视化，时间/技术有限的数据新闻记者；或者想做深度数据报道的小型新闻编辑部比较实用

#AI记者 #AI新闻 #Data2Story

user.name: Jeffrey Towson 陶迅
user.description: I help increase online growth and strengthen digital AI moats. Digital & AI mgmt consultant. Get my free email and Tech Strategy podcast.

fullText:
4 COOL BATTERY INNOVATIONS BY CATL

Here are some innovations and takeaways from my visit to CATL:

 1: Cell-to-Pack (CTP) Bypasses Modules To Maximize Energy Density

CTP eliminates internal module boxes entirely and placing cells directly into the pack wrapper

CATL's Qilin architecture frees up space to achieve a 72% volume utilization efficiency.

 2: Hybrid Battery Chemistry Solves Cold-Weather Power Drops

The Freevoy system mixes different chemistries inside a single casing.

This pairs high-energy lithium cells with cold-resistant sodium cells

This creates a fallback to maintain performance in freezing temperatures.

 3: Manufacturing Scale Enables Big, Ongoing R&D

CATL commands dominant market share

So their 10% of revenue R&D allocation allows them to outspend rivals on structural and chemical engineering simultaneously.

Read here for more. 

https://t.co/pUpcfwcRxA

fullText:
We need governance built for the agentic era.

As agents increasingly act on enterprise data, governance can no longer stop at access. It has to control what AI can do, give agents the context they need to understand the business, and offer the choice to work across clouds, regions, formats, and platforms.

Today, we're advancing Unity Catalog with:
- Unity AI Gateway to govern models, agents, tools, MCPs, and their runtime interactions
- Glossary and Domains to create a shared semantic foundation for business concepts and context
- Cross-cloud, cross-region governance and expanded interoperability across open formats and more

fullText:
Today, we enable AutoResearch in the physical world for the first time! Introducing ENPIRE: we give 8 Codex agents a fleet of robots, an allocation of GPUs, and generous token budget. We set them free with a simple goal: solve the task as quickly as possible, keep the robots busy but stay safe, don't waste precious compute. Make no mistake.

Then humans step aside and our watch begins. The robot fleet starts to come alive: they learn to look for visual clues, reset the scene, practice novel skills, tinker with control stack, read papers online, debate, reflect, get stuck, and try again directly on the hardware. All we did is to give Codex an API to the world of atoms, and the rest is emergence.

ENPIRE is able to solve high-precision tasks like tying zip-ties, organizing fine pins, and installing GPUs all by itself. We also discovered a new type of "physical scaling": 8 robots exploring in parallel improves significantly faster than fewer ones. 

A part of our NVIDIA GEAR lab now self-improves tirelessly over night. We just read the reports in the morning. 

/goal: we all take a holiday and Jensen wouldn't even notice ;)

We will be open-sourcing everything, so you can host your self-running robot lab at home too! Deep dive in the thread:

user.name: Elon Musk
user.description: https://t.co/dDtDyVssfm

fullText: It is humbling to consider that if we harness just 1 millionth of the Sun’s power for AI, that will be much more than a million times the intelligence of all of humanity

user.name: elvis
user.description: Building self-improving AI @dair_ai • Prev: Meta AI | PhD • Learn about AI Agents for FREE here: https://t.co/P5SA9u54xO

fullText:
Verifiers are a big deal.

Without good verifiers, /goal & /loop breaks a lot.

Anything out of distribution for an LLM, the agent will struggle to verify work correctly.

I think it's worth tuning your own verifiers and figuring out how to hook them up with your current agents.
